title: Etsy earnings Q1 2025
text: Etsy reported mixed Q1 2025 results with revenue beating expectations but GMS continuing to lag year-over-year. Revenue reached $651.2 million, exceeding the $643 million consensus forecast, while adjusted earnings per share came in at $0.46 versus expectations of $0.49. Consolidated GMS declined 6.5% year-over-year to $2.8 billion, though the core Etsy marketplace reached $2.46 billion, up 5.5% year-over-year, marking the second consecutive quarter of marketplace growth. The company took a $101.7 million non-recurring charge related to the sale of Reverb, its music gear marketplace. Etsy announced the planned $1.2 billion sale of Depop to eBay, expected to close by the end of Q3 2026. Management emphasized that these divestitures allow Etsy to focus on higher-return capital allocation in the core marketplace. The company highlighted AI investments in personalization and search, with generative AI ranking improving buyer experience. Agentic commerce partnerships generated fifteen-fold year-over-year growth in agentic traffic, with integrations across ChatGPT, Google, and Microsoft platforms.
